__metadata.name: Lithium Supply Chain Atlas ŌĆö Flow Dataset
methodology_notes: Every volume estimate is anchored to a cited published tonnage figure or explicitly marked as bounded estimate with uncertainty.
Mining ŌåÆ Refining flows use actual trade data where published (ABS for Australia, Cochilco for Chile, Chinese customs via Mysteel where available).

Refining ŌåÆ Cells flows are largely not independently traded ŌĆö China's integrated supply chain means intra-Chinese flow is the largest single movement but does not show up in bilateral trade data. Cross-border refining-to-cells flows are small in volume but carry significant policy-weight.

Volume units: kt LCE (lithium carbonate equivalent, thousand tonnes). Concentrate converted from physical tonnage using 6% Li2O spodumene ŌåÆ ~0.101 t LCE per tonne concentrate. Direct chemical imports (carbonate, hydroxide) reported in LCE-equivalent per industry standard.

For the CHNŌåÆCHN intra-flow, the 650 kt central estimate represents domestic-refined Chinese chemicals flowing to Chinese cell plants. A separate ~240 kt flow of imported carbonate (from Chile/Argentina) also terminates at Chinese cell plants but is rendered in the atlas as part of the CHLŌåÆCHN and ARGŌåÆCHN flows with a tooltip explainer noting the downstream destination.

Plant-level refining flows (Kwinana, Kemerton, Gwangyang, Bitterfeld, B├®cancour, Naraha) use disclosed 2024 actual output rather than nameplate capacity. Per Query 7, country-level refining shares below top-three are not publicly verifiable.

policy_state_legend.qualifying: Flow + destination meet OBBBA PFE rules and source is FTA partner or critical-minerals-agreement partner ŌĆö eligible for US ┬¦45X/┬¦45Y/┬¦48E credit contribution.
policy_state_legend.blocked: Flow disqualified from US credit contribution due to PFE origin (Chinese-refined or Chinese-owned entity, regardless of geography).
policy_state_legend.ambiguous: PFE classification depends on Treasury guidance not yet issued; deals proceeding with indemnity packages.
policy_state_legend.pending_regs: Statutorily triggered but operational tests not yet defined in regulations; effective boundary pending future IRS/Treasury rules (expected late 2026 per Notice 2026-15).
policy_state_legend.blocked_once_refined: Flow is itself legal and falls under no US policy restriction at origin, but resulting refined product becomes PFE-blocked at destination. Rendered in atlas as gradient from neutral at source to policy-color at destination.
policy_state_legend.host_state_controls: Flow originates in jurisdiction with export restriction, state-partnership mandate, or resource-nationalism regime. May overlap with other tags.
policy_state_legend.eu_crma: Flow terminates in EU refining/cells and is subject to CRMA strategic-project provisions or 65% third-country ceiling from 2030.

__metadata.chinese_ownership_node_marker: Atlas applies a distinct node-marker overlay (muted purple) to all majority-Chinese-owned nodes regardless of physical location. Covers: CHN_mine, CHN_ref, CHN_cell (domestic), ZWE, NAM (Chinese operators), AUS_ref_kwinana (Tianqi 51%), IDN_cell (partial, Chinese-controlled portions like IWIP). Overlay signals PFE exposure for US-bound downstream material.
__metadata.bankable_findings_reference: 10 verified findings stored in memory entries 15-18 for launch LinkedIn post.

flows.note: The defining flow of the global lithium system. 1.86 Mt spodumene H1 2025 per ABS. Australia's share of Chinese spodumene imports fell from 64.3% (2024) to 50.1% (Jan-Sep 2025) as African supply rose. Volume annualized from H1 2025 ├Ś 2. Flow is legal and FTA-origin, but resulting Chinese-refined chemicals are PFE-blocked for US credit contribution.
sources: ABS trade data via Argus Media Aug 2025 (1H 2025: 1.86 Mt)
Mysteel Jan-Sep 2025 China imports analysis (50.1% Australia share)

flows.note: Greenbushes spodumene feeding Tianqi Kwinana hydroxide plant. **CRITICAL: Flow is legal but destination is 51% Chinese-owned (Tianqi), so classified SFE under OBBBA. Flow output does NOT count toward US MACR compliance.** Kwinana Train 1 produced only 3,500 t LiOH FY2024; Train 2 construction halted. IGO booked A$955m loss FY2025. Atlas tooltip should flag this as the single most consequential PFE reclassification vs conventional wisdom.
sources: The West Australian reporting on Kwinana 2024 output

flows.note: Feeds Albemarle Kemerton lithium hydroxide refinery. Nameplate 100 kt/yr (4 trains); 2024 progression saw Trains 2/3/4 progressively shelved, Train 1 subsequently idled. Actual 2024 output materially below nameplate. Albemarle is US-owned so ownership test clear; geography test clear. Flow PFE-qualifying throughout. Uncertainty range reflects ambiguity about actual operational output in 2024.
sources: Reuters/CNBC Jul 31 2024 ŌĆö Albemarle Kemerton production cuts
ABC News Aug 1 2024 ŌĆö Kemerton job losses announcement

flows.commodity: spodumene concentrate (routed for Chinese-operated Indonesian refining)
flows.note: Emerging flow, Indonesia overtook South Korea as #2 Australian spodumene destination H1 2025 (66,678 t vs 25,098 t prior year). Chengxin Lithium (Chinese) commissioned 60 kt/yr lithium plant in Indonesia mid-2024 processing hard-rock imports. **Per Query 8/OBBBA: the Chinese-controlled Indonesian processing is structurally ineligible for US credit compliance despite non-China geography.** Classic case of 'China+1' strategy that doesn't clear PFE.
sources: Argus Media Aug 2025 ŌĆö Indonesia overtakes Korea

flows.note: Pilbara Minerals spodumene ŌåÆ POSCO-Pilbara Gwangyang JV. **Important reality check: Gwangyang produced only 1,965 t LiOH in Q3 2024 from Train 1; Train 2 commissioning began Dec 2024. Actual 2024 utilization was far below nameplate.** This ramp gap explains why Korean retreat from Chilean carbonate imports has not yet been compensated by Gwangyang output ŌĆö the substitution is aspirational, not achieved. Korea-US FTA qualifying.
sources: Fastmarkets Nov 2024 ŌĆö POSCO Train 1 production data

flows.note: **Largest single refiningŌåÆcells cross-border flow.** Chile's 2024 lithium exports: $2.895 bn total, 71% to China by value (=$2.057 bn, ~186 kt LCE). Flow goes directly from Chilean refiners to Chinese cell plants (via Chinese distributors). Represents the 'imported passthrough' component that contributes to Chinese cell production ŌĆö important for understanding that China is a net importer of refined lithium despite 70% refining share. Classified 'blocked' because destination is Chinese cell production (downstream ineligible for US credits).

**Korea's quiet retreat from Chile.** Korea's share of Chilean lithium exports fell from 28% (2023) to 15% (2024); value fell 76% to $434m. Korean cell makers are substituting Australian-spodumene-refined-in-Korea (Gwangyang) but POSCO ramp has been slow (see AUS_GWANGYANG_ore flow). In 2024 Korean makers were likely running lean inventory or sourcing elsewhere. Intended trajectory: reduce Chilean dependency further as Gwangyang ramps. PFE-qualifying throughout.
sources: Argus Media Q1 2025 Chilean lithium exports

flows.note: **Chile overtook Argentina as #1 US lithium source.** USGS MCS 2026 reports 2021-24 US import sources: Chile 54%, Argentina 43%, other 3%. Reversal of 2019-22 pattern. The rebalancing strengthens US 30D compliance since Chile is an FTA partner. Volume calculated from 4.2% of Chile exports = ~$119m; LCE-equivalent at 2024 prices. Per Query 2: total US lithium imports for consumption 2024 = ~3,020 t Li content (~16 kt LCE), of which Chile ~54% = ~8.6 kt LCE ŌĆö slight tension with the share-based 7 kt figure, reflecting published-data inconsistency across sources.
sources: USGS MCS 2026 ŌĆö US Import Sources 2021-24

flows.note: Feeds Japanese chemical sector (Nippon Chemical, Toyota Tsusho-Arcadium JV). Japan runs on NMC chemistry; Chilean battery-grade carbonate feeds this. Relatively stable share through 2024.

**EU CRMA undermined from the demand side.** Historically flowed to Belgium (Umicore CAM operations). Q1 2025: -67% YoY after Umicore CORE strategy halved 2025 cathode production. Pre-2025 Belgium was Chile's #3 destination; now collapsed. Destination 'DEU' in atlas is simplification ŌĆö actual flow was Belgium for CAM processing, then CAM outputs to German cell plants. Tool tooltip should explain this.
sources: Argus Media Q1 2025 ŌĆö Belgium 67% drop
Umicore CORE strategy 2025 guidance + Full Year Results 2025

flows.note: **Major correction from v0.1 (was 48.8 kt LCE ŌĆö 7x overstatement).** Corrected to ~5.9 kt LCE per Query 2: 43% ├Ś 3,020 t Li content ├Ś 5.323 LCE factor = ~6.9 kt LCE. **Per Query 8: classification moved from 'qualifying' to 'ambiguous' ŌĆö Rio Tinto Arcadium closed March 2025; Chinalco owns 14.99% of Rio Tinto (below 25% single-owner FIE trigger but vulnerable if aggregate-ownership test adopted in future Treasury rules).** Flow proceeds pending regulatory clarification. Rio Tinto's 200 kt LCE by 2028 ambition depends on ┬¦45X eligibility.
sources: USGS MCS 2026 ŌĆö US Import Sources 2021-24 (Argentina 43%)
IRS Notice 2026-15 ŌĆö FIE ownership tests pending

flows.note: Ganfeng's Cauchari-Olaroz and Mariana operations ship chemicals to Chinese parent. Volume estimate reflects 50% of Argentine refined output ├Ś 2024 volume. Part of the 241 kt LCE refined carbonate flowing into Chinese cell production. Even though flow is Argentina-origin, destination is Chinese cells ŌåÆ PFE-blocked for US credit contribution.
POSCO-Salta and Allkem offtake flows. Feeds Korean cathode ecosystem. Qualifying via US-Korea FTA; Argentina source is ambiguous at absolute level but flow-to-Korea path is clean.
sources: POSCO 2024 annual report ŌĆö Salta brine project

flows.commodity: spodumene concentrate (post-beneficiation, subject to regime flux)
flows.note: **The Zimbabwe paradox intensified.** 2024 volume from USGS 22,000 t Li ├Ś 5.323 = 117 kt LCE, adjusted for operational disruptions and export routing = ~105 kt LCE. All operators Chinese-owned (Bikita=Sinomine, Arcadia=Huayou, Sabi Star=Chengxin) EXCEPT Zulu (Premier African Minerals UK-listed, Canmax 13% equity). **Regulatory escalation in real time:** Dec 2022 raw ore ban ŌåÆ June 2025 concentrate ban announcement ŌåÆ Feb 25 2026 immediate suspension of all concentrate exports ŌåÆ April 2026 quota system with processing-commitment conditions. 2026 tiered export tax: 10% raw ore, 10% concentrate, 0% lithium sulphate ŌĆö explicit value-addition policy. Chinese operators being forced downstream.
sources: African Climate Wire March 2026 ŌĆö tiered export tax

flows.note: Sigma Lithium Grota do Cirilo concentrate ships predominantly to China. 2024 volume nearly doubled from 2023 (USGS MCS 2025). BYD + LG Energy Solution offtake arrangements forming. Brazilian government $2.1bn by 2030 investment in domestic capacity.

flows.commodity: spodumene concentrate (LGES offtake, routed through Korean refining)
flows.note: Small but strategically important. Sigma-LGES MOU 2024 for direct supply to North American gigafactories via Korean refining (LGES is Korean, US-FTA qualifying). **Per Query 8: Brazil has no US FTA, so 30D qualifying status depends on MACR-threshold interpretation not yet operationalized ŌĆö classified 'pending_regs'.** Flow volume is growing but uncertain.
sources: IRS Notice 2026-15 MACR framework (cost tables pending end-2026)

flows.note: Nemaska Lithium vertically integrated Whabouchi mine ŌåÆ B├®cancour hydroxide plant. Canada-US FTA (USMCA) qualifying. Ramp-up 2024-2025; 2024 actual output modest per plant-level estimate.
Albemarle Silver Peak brine ŌåÆ Kings Mountain and Silver Peak carbonate. Small but PFE-qualifying throughout. USGS withholds precise figures.
sources: USGS MCS 2025 ŌĆö US production withheld

flows.note: Xinfeng Investments (Chinese) operates largest Namibian lithium operations. Similar structural pattern to Zimbabwe ŌĆö nominal export restriction regime but Chinese ownership routes concentrate to Chinese refining.
sources: Namibian Ministry of Mines export controls June 2023

flows: // ŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉ REFINING ŌåÆ CELLS (INTRA-COUNTRY AND CROSS-BORDER) ŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉ

flows.note: **Largest single flow in the system ŌĆö 4x larger than next-biggest.** Corrected to range 580-720 kt LCE per Query 1 mass balance reconciliation (central estimate 650). Uncertainty reflects 2024 inventory build during price crash (~100-200 kt LCE accumulated). **Critical context: China was NET IMPORTER of refined lithium in 2024 despite 70% refining share.** Chinese cell plants also received ~240 kt LCE of imported refined carbonate from Chile/Argentina ŌĆö that flow is captured separately in CHL_CHN_chem and ARG_CHN_chem. Chokepoint is processing concentration, not self-sufficiency.
sources: MIIT/CABIA Feb 2025 release ŌĆö 691 kt Li2CO3 + 354 kt LiOH
IEA Global Critical Minerals Outlook 2025 ŌĆö 70% refining share
China Customs 2024 ŌĆö 235 kt Li2CO3 imports

flows.note: Korean cell makers source significant share of lithium chemicals from China despite domestic POSCO ramp-up efforts. Affected by China's Nov 2025 battery technology export controls (technical transfer more than volume, but material for Korean planning).
sources: Korean Ministry of Trade battery supply chain data
IEA Oct 2025 commentary on China Nov 2025 export controls

flows.note: Japanese cell makers (Panasonic) source from China for NMC chemistry feedstock. Smaller than Korean equivalent.
sources: Japanese Ministry of Economy supply chain data

flows.note: Chinese chemicals flow to EU cell plants, with Hungary as proxy destination (CATL Debrecen + Samsung SDI G├Čd + SK On clusters). CRMA 65% third-country ceiling creates long-term pressure from 2030 but no current constraint. Encodes as HUN in atlas but represents EU-wide flow. CATL Debrecen mass cell production starts early 2026.
sources: EU CRMA ŌĆö 65% third-country supply threshold from 2030

flows.note: **The defining blocked flow.** Pre-IRA this was a significant route. Under OBBBA PFE rules + 30D FEOC, Chinese-refined lithium in US-credit-eligible batteries is disqualified. Volume has dropped since 2023 and continues to decline as developers restructure. Rendered in atlas as dashed policy-blue flow line. Ford/CATL Marshall licensing arrangement is the notable exception ŌĆö codified 'Ford controls; CATL merely trains' distinction survived OBBBA final revisions.
sources: IRS Notice 2026-15 ŌĆö Feb 12 2026 MACR framework
Ford BlueOval Marshall MI arrangement codified in final OBBBA revisions

flows.note: POSCO Gwangyang ŌåÆ LG/Samsung/SK On. Korea-US FTA qualifying. **Volume reflects POSCO ramp reality: only ~8 kt total 2024 output at Gwangyang, of which majority consumed domestically.** Growing flow but small in absolute terms.

AMG Bitterfeld Module 1 (20 kt/y) commissioned September 2024. Limited 2024 output. EU CRMA Strategic Project. Represents European refining ambition, but still ~1% of global. Rock Tech Guben converter under construction, production expected 2026.
sources: AMG Critical Materials press release Sept 2024

flows.note: Naraha (Rio Tinto/Toyota Tsusho, 10 kt/y, operational 2023) + Toyotsu operations. Feedstock imported from Argentina. Japan-US relationship close but no FTA ŌĆö PFE classification ambiguous pending Treasury interpretation of ally-based cases.

Tesla Nevada, Georgia SK On, Michigan LG, Indiana StarPlus operations partly fed by Albemarle domestic chemicals. Small absolute terms but fully PFE-qualifying. Growing through 2025 with StarPlus ramping.

Stellantis-Samsung SDI JV Plant 1 at Kokomo operational 2025. Fully PFE-qualifying: Samsung (Korea FTA), Stellantis (Italian-French-Dutch). Represents template for 'ally-shoring' JV structure that sidesteps OBBBA PFE while leveraging Korean cell technology + US market access.
Small volume but fully integrated USMCA supply chain. NextStar Windsor (LGES sole owner from Feb 2026) now serving both EV cell and BESS markets.

Small residual flow to Canadian assembly (NextStar Windsor) from Chinese chemicals. PFE-blocked for ┬¦45X/30D credit eligibility but plants continue operational. Volume declining as CanadaŌåÆB├®cancour vertical integration ramps.

__key_reconciliation_vs_v0.1: ARG_USA_chem: 48.8 ŌåÆ 5.9 kt LCE (7x correction per Query 2)
CHN_CHN_chem: point estimate 720 ŌåÆ range 580-720 kt (central 650) per Query 1
AUS_KWINANA_ore: policy state qualifying ŌåÆ BLOCKED (per Query 8 SFE classification)
ARG_USA_chem: policy state qualifying ŌåÆ ambiguous (Rio Tinto Chinalco 14.99% exposure)
BRA_USA_ore: policy state ambiguous ŌåÆ pending_regs (no FTA, MACR thresholds pending)
AUS_IDN_ore: policy state previously not tagged ŌåÆ blocked_once_refined (Chengxin China-controlled)
Korea-Chile flow: reframed with POSCO ramp gap context ŌĆö substitution aspirational not achieved
Zimbabwe flow: annotated with Feb 2026/April 2026 regulatory escalation
CHL period citation: H1 2025 ŌåÆ Jan-Sep 2025 on Mysteel Australia share data
China refining share: 65% ŌåÆ 70% per IEA GCMO 2025, corrected throughout
All cell manufacturing volumes corrected per Query 6 (China 2,450 GWh / 85%, US 185, Hungary 35, France 13, Sweden 0, Canada 5)
__critical_unresolved_items_for_build: Ford/CATL Michigan licensing arrangement: not rendered as distinct flow since it's a technology licensing relationship, not a lithium flow. Consider adding as a 'technology transfer' annotation on the Michigan node.
Korean retreat from Chile: flow CHL_KOR_chem is annotated but the *trend* (declining share) is not visually captured. Consider time-series option for future version.
Inventory build: 100-200 kt LCE accumulated 2024 is not a 'flow' but is material. Currently embedded in CHN_CHN range only. Could add as annotation on CHN_cell node.
Ambiguous/pending_regs states: need distinct visual treatment in overlay JSON (suggest: dashed line for blocked, dotted for ambiguous, dashed with question-mark marker for pending_regs).
NovAndino Litio JV (Codelco-SQM, operational Dec 27 2025): not explicitly represented as separate node; folded into CHL/CHL_ref. Could be added as production milestone annotation.
